# Khobar Aquifer

**Summary**: Geological formation studied in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] studied the Khobar Aquifer

- [[2024-ahmad|Ahmad, 2024]] characterized the khobar-aquifer as a key water source for irrigation.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] modeled the flow and solute transport within the Khobar aquifer.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] characterized the Khobar aquifer's hydrogeochemistry and suitability for use.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2024-ahmad|Ahmad, 2024]] characterized the khobar-aquifer as a key water source for irrigation.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] modeled the flow and solute transport within the Khobar aquifer.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] characterized the Khobar aquifer's hydrogeochemistry and suitability for use.

## Reservoir Properties



## Key Findings Across Sources

- studied the Khobar Aquifer ([[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]])

- The Khobar aquifer is characterized by fractured limestone with an 80m thickness and a depth of around 130m, with a transmissivity ranging from 0.9 x 10^-2 m^2/s to 0.29 m^2/s. ([[2024-ahmad|Ahmad, 2024, p. 20]])

- Water quality in some parts of the Khobar aquifer was found to be good, with concentrations less than 500 ppm. ([[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995, p. 28]])

- The aquifer has a thickness of 20-70m with an average transmissivity of 2.9 x 10^-3 m^2/s. ([[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992, p. 22]])
